weather_report:
Subiaco wakes under a quilt of broken cloud, the sun slipping through in pearly sheets that cast soft, forgiving shadows across verandas and shopfronts. The air feels comfortable rather than stern, the view clear, with that crisp mid-morning definition that makes colors behave. Through late morning into early afternoon the sky keeps its dappled rhythm, brightness arriving in generous spells before a veil drifts back across it. Expect a mostly dry, pleasantly tempered day; the UV, however, will gather strength toward midday even with the cloud, so treat it with respect.

Dress for ease: light fabrics, sleeves you can roll, sunglasses, a brimmed hat, and a calm swipe of sunscreen. It’s the sort of day for an unhurried circuit of Rokeby Road to pick up a loaf and something green, a coffee tucked beneath an awning, or a quiet hour at the library before a lunch break walk when the light goes soft again. If home beckons, throw the windows open and tend to balcony plants while the sun plays hide-and-seek, letting the day move around you rather than through you.
